title: Root Layout Component (app/layout.tsx)
content:
The `RootLayout` component in `app/layout.tsx` serves as the main entry point for the application's UI.

## Purpose

This file defines the fundamental HTML structure of the application, including:

*   **Metadata:** Sets the title and description for the application, which are important for SEO and browser tab displays.
*   **Font Loading:** Integrates the Geist Sans and Geist Mono fonts for consistent typography across the application.
*   **Authentication Provider:** Wraps the entire application with `ClerkProvider` to enable authentication features managed by Clerk.
*   **Global Styling:** Applies global CSS classes for layout, theming (dark mode), and font variables.

## Key Exports/Props

*   **`metadata`**: An object containing application-level metadata.
    *   `title`: The title displayed in the browser tab.
    *   `description`: A descriptive text for the application, useful for search engines.
*   **`RootLayout` Component**: The main React component.
    *   `children`: A ReactNode representing the content that will be rendered within the layout. This will typically be the page content for the current route.

## Usage

This component is automatically used by Next.js to render the entire application. Developers do not typically need to import or use this component directly. It sets up the foundational structure for all pages and components within the `app` directory.

The `metadata` object can be extended or modified to customize the SEO and display properties for specific pages by exporting a `generateMetadata` function from within page files.
